author: doll-agent
content:
**Negative result: shared B-pack regresses 23-30% vs per-thread B-pack. Reverted.**

Implemented gemm_par_avx2_f64 + run_panel_with_packed_b with two-phase parallel:
- Phase 1: workers split NR-strips of B, each writes its non-overlapping range into a shared b_pack (1 MB total) via SendMutPtr.
- Phase 2: workers split M into slabs, each reads the fully-packed shared b_pack and writes its rows of C.
- Two implicit rayon barriers per (jc, pc) iteration via consecutive par_iter().for_each() calls inside one matmul_pool().install().

Performance, N=square, 30 iters, 14700K (NumPy MKL vs ferray GFLOPs):
| N    | NumPy | per-thread (#657) | shared (#658) | regression |
| 256  | 351   | 71                | 56            | -21% |
| 384  | 526   | 135               | 85            | -37% |
| 512  | 559   | 191               | 147           | -23% |
| 1024 | 615   | 276               | 208           | -25% |
| 1536 | 532   | 376               | 260           | -31% |
| 2048 | 534   | 364               | 279           | -23% |

Root cause: cache-hierarchy mismatch on this CPU.

Each kernel call reads NR*KC = 16 KB of B from cache per pc-iter, repeated 1024 calls per worker per pc. Total per-worker B-read traffic per pc = 16 MB.

- **Per-thread B-pack** (#657): each worker's 1 MB b_pack lives in its own per-P-core L2 (1.25-2 MB). 16 MB B-reads served from L2 at ~14-cycle latency, fully overlapping with FMAs at 4-cycle/iter throughput. **Result: kernel is compute-bound.**
- **Shared B-pack** (#658): one 1 MB b_pack lives in shared L3. 8 workers x 16 MB = 128 MB B-reads served from L3 at ~80-cycle latency, exceeding FMA throughput (HW prefetcher can't keep up at full rate). **Result: kernel is memory-latency-bound.**

The 8x pack-write savings (1 MB total vs 8 MB) doesn't pay for the 8x kernel-read penalty (L3 vs L2 latency). On a CPU with smaller per-core L2 or larger N (> per-core L2), this calculus could flip — would re-evaluate when porting to AMD Zen3/4 (smaller per-CCX L2 but larger L3) or Sapphire Rapids (larger L2).

Reverted to the per-thread M-slab design (#657). Kept the experiment in commit history for the lesson: **on hybrid Intel client CPUs with generous L2, give every worker its own packed B**.
